title: Why the Khashabiyyah Came to Mecca
description: According to Hishim [b. al-Kalbij-Abu Mikhnaf and `Ali b. Muhammad [al-Madi 'inij-Maslamah b. Muhirib, the reason was as follows: `Abdallah b. al-Zubayr imprisoned Muhammad b. al-Hanafiyyah and those members of his family who were with him, together with seventeen notables from al-Kufah, at Zamzam.231 Unwilling to swear allegiance to someone upon whom the community (ummah) had not united in agreement, 228. Cf. Qur'an 7: 87, ro: ro9, and r2:8o. 229. Leiden note suggests that one should read "He said...." 230. For the origin of the name, see note 207.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 21
